jiang_claim: Catholics are not allowed to read the Bible; Protestants must read the Bible — contrast as absolute.
mainstream_anchor: Catholic teaching and practice (modern): laity are encouraged to read Scripture; historical restriction was context-specific (Trent-era vernacular bans), not 'never read'.
mainstream_summary: Contemporary Catholicism presents scripture as central; access and translation were historically contested but not equivalent to 'forbidden to read' in the lecture's sense.
evidence_notes: Lecture may compress Reformation polemics; compare to Catechism of the Catholic Church on Scripture, magisterium, and laity; cite primary sources for book. YouTube comments push back on 'Catholics cannot read the Bible'.

jiang_claim: The United States, in its 'soul' and founding purpose, is a Christian nation oriented to Bible supremacy and building the kingdom of God on Earth (vs secular multicultural surface).
mainstream_anchor: US historiography (plural): founding includes Enlightenment/deist strands, religious pluralism, and later constitutional disestablishment; 'Christian nation' is politically contested.
mainstream_summary: Many historians emphasize mixed motives and conflict over establishment; civil religion and evangelical influence are real but not the only accredited narrative of 'what America is'.
evidence_notes: Useful to map against Bellah (civil religion), denominational splits; avoid single-sentence verdict in the book.

jiang_claim: Dispensationalist / Christian-Zionist actors are emphasized as drivers of Israel–Palestine / Israel–Iran tension and US alignment.
mainstream_anchor: Mainstream IR and policy analysis: security dilemmas, alliances, energy, domestic politics, regional state interests — religion one factor among many.
mainstream_summary: Academic and many policy accounts weight state interest, threat perception, and alliance structures; religious lobbies matter but scope varies by author.
evidence_notes: Not mutually exclusive with structural IR; divergence is weighting and causal priority. Good book section: layered causes.

jiang_claim: US invasion of Iran is treated as a serious eventual baseline; specific windows and Trump-conditional forecasts offered.
mainstream_anchor: Western official messaging and much press (2023–2025): 'all options' rhetoric exists but full-scale invasion often treated as high-cost / not default baseline.
mainstream_summary: Open debate; many analysts stress deterrence, sanctions, limited strikes vs full invasion — consensus on inevitability is weak.
evidence_notes: Pair with prediction registry rows; mainstream itself shifted with events — date-stamp any 'mainstream' summary.

jiang_claim: US GDP is about 'hundred trillion' dollars; debt ~third of that (in lecture's arithmetic).
mainstream_anchor: US nominal GDP is on the order of tens of trillions USD (BEA / World Bank), not ~100T; ratio check fails if numerator is debt ~34T.
mainstream_summary: Likely ASR or slip: 'hundred' misheard or misspoken; verify before any scholarly use.
evidence_notes: Cross-check debt-to-GDP with official series; preserve transcript verbatim in archive.

jiang_claim: Pursuit of happiness (Jefferson) means amassing wealth as life's fundamental purpose in the lecture's gloss.
mainstream_anchor: Intellectual history: contested readings (virtue, property, eudaimonia vs narrow wealth); not a single academic consensus equating phrase to 'maximize money'.
mainstream_summary: Simplified pedagogical equation; historians of ideas would qualify heavily.
evidence_notes: Label as interpretive gloss for class, not as settled founding scholarship.

jiang_claim: Chinese workers' wages have not gone up for the past 30–40 years (elite capture story).
mainstream_anchor: Development economists document large real wage growth and poverty reduction over decades; inequality/elite capture also debated — not a flat 'no wage growth' consensus.
mainstream_summary: Heterodox vs mainstream empirical dispute; needs dated data series.
evidence_notes: If book cites, use World Bank/Penn World Table or comparable; lecture uses broad brush.

jiang_claim: Iran's major enemy in the Middle East is Saudi Arabia; many think it is Israel.
mainstream_anchor: Policy analysis typically treats Israel–Iran and Gulf–Iran tensions as both severe; ranking 'primary enemy' varies by issue (nuclear, proxies, ideology).
mainstream_summary: Lecture elevates Saudi–Iran dyad for narrative; not a universal consensus that Israel is secondary.
evidence_notes: Book can present as one analytical map among several.

jiang_claim: On Sunni vs Shia difference: 'no one actually knows' — then gives succession dispute gloss.
mainstream_anchor: Islamic studies: sectarian differences are heavily documented (law, history, ritual); 'unknowable' is rhetorical compression for a high school class.
mainstream_summary: Pedagogical simplification + hook; not a scholarly claim about epistemic limits.
evidence_notes: Label as classroom shorthand in any serious treatment.

jiang_claim: Pentagon three-option menu story; MBS on Kushner 'in my pocket'; PIF $2B to Kushner fund — as evidence Saudis steer US Iran policy toward escalation.
mainstream_anchor: Reporting and investigations vary; some claims disputed, some confirmed in part; legal/regulatory scrutiny of fund.
mainstream_summary: High-stakes interpretive narrative; cite named investigations if book uses.
evidence_notes: Separate verified facts from lecture's causal spin.

jiang_claim: 2020 margin of victory was only ~65,000 votes in key battleground states (flip that many and Trump would have won).
mainstream_anchor: Certified state margins and swing totals vary by definition (which states count as tipping-set); '65k' is a common shorthand tied to specific post-hoc analyses, not a single official line item.
mainstream_summary: Pedagogical compression; verify against FEC/state certified counts before scholarly citation.
evidence_notes: Not necessarily false — depends on counterfactual definition; label as illustrative margin talk.

jiang_claim: Trump-as-Russian-asset narrative was a 'complete lie' / hoax (Russiagate framing).
mainstream_anchor: Mueller report and bipartisan Senate Intel findings are complex: documented Russian interference and multiple contacts; 'asset' is contested political language, not a single judicial verdict of treason.
mainstream_summary: Lecture uses prosecutorial shorthand; mainstream journalism and official reports are mixed, not uniformly 'hoax' vs uniformly 'proven traitor'.
evidence_notes: Book chapter: separate interference facts, legal outcomes, and rhetorical labels.

jiang_claim: At the height of the Vietnam War, three million US soldiers had to be deployed (alongside peak ~500,000 in country).
mainstream_anchor: Vietnam-era personnel statistics: commonly cited peaks are on the order of ~500,000 troops in Vietnam at one time; cumulative US military personnel who served in theater over the war is often cited around millions total, but 'three million deployed at the height' is not standard phrasing.
mainstream_summary: Pedagogical magnification or conflation of cumulative service with simultaneous deployment; verify before scholarly use.
evidence_notes: Compare DOD/VVA figures for served vs in-country peak.

jiang_claim: Shock and awe means you no longer need consent of the people or Congressional approval to fight war; SOF can act without oversight.
mainstream_anchor: US constitutional and statutory framework still imposes War Powers, appropriations, and command authorities; SOF operations remain contested legally and politically — not a clean 'no oversight' baseline.
mainstream_summary: Lecture states maximal independence thesis; real governance is disputed and historically punctuated by scandals, reporting, and congressional pressure.
evidence_notes: Book: distinguish classroom causal story from legal-institutional detail.

jiang_claim: For every ship America makes, China can make 300 ships (manufacturing capacity contrast).
mainstream_anchor: Naval production comparisons are highly sensitive to ship class, yard capacity, and time window; '300:1' is not a standard cited ratio in serious defense accounting without heavy qualification.
mainstream_summary: Rhetorical ratio for class effect; replace with dated sources if used in book.
evidence_notes: Use navalists' production studies; avoid single headline ratio.

jiang_claim: IRGC has strongest motive to remove Raisi so Mojtaba Khamenei can succeed and the IRGC retains economic monopoly; helicopter crash may be staged internal 'accident'.
mainstream_anchor: Official investigations and open sources treated the crash as an accident; attribution to IRGC is speculative and disputed; no public forensic proof in Western/Iranian reporting as of routine coverage.
mainstream_summary: Game-theory scenario, not established fact; book must label as counterfactual branch.
evidence_notes: Lecture explicitly disclaims evidence; keep scenario separate from newsroom consensus.

jiang_claim: Named embassy hostage-era students (e.g. Dehghan, Jafari, Bagheri) became top military leaders as described.
mainstream_anchor: Biographies and career timelines are checkable; some connections may be simplified or contested in detail.
mainstream_summary: Verify each name and role before scholarly use; ASR garbles names.
evidence_notes: Cross-check Persian/English sources for each career.

jiang_claim: IRGC controls about 10–50% of the entire Iranian economy.
mainstream_anchor: Estimates of IRGC economic share vary widely; ranges and methodologies differ across sources.
mainstream_summary: Wide bracket reflects uncertainty; cite specific studies if used.
evidence_notes: Prefer peer-reviewed or major think-tank estimates with date.

jiang_claim: Ukrainian military includes neo-Nazis who push maximal war; Russian framing of 'Nazis' as anti-Russian killers.
mainstream_anchor: Western and Ukrainian accounts typically distinguish Azov/regiment histories, far-right fringe, and Russian 'denazification' justification; consensus is not 'Ukraine run by Nazis'.
mainstream_summary: Heavily contested; overlaps Russian state narrative. Book must separate factual units from polemic.
evidence_notes: See e.g. ADL, academic work on Azov and far-right; lecture uses classroom shorthand.

jiang_claim: Israel's optimal outcome is US and Iran both destroyed / attrited so Israel becomes regional 'top dog'; Saudi same.
mainstream_anchor: Realist analysis emphasizes Israeli and Saudi dependence on US security guarantees; 'optimal mutual destruction of US and Iran' is not standard policy scholarship.
mainstream_summary: Pedagogical game-theory exaggeration for class; not a forecast of official strategy.
evidence_notes: Use as scenario logic, not attribution of intent to states.

jiang_claim: AIPAC (lecture: 'APAC') is second-most-powerful US lobbying org after pensioners' lobby (~30M members).
mainstream_anchor: Lobby influence rankings vary by methodology (spending, legislative outcomes); 'second after retirees' is not a standard cited league table.
mainstream_summary: Illustrative ranking; verify with OpenSecrets or comparable if cited.
evidence_notes: ASR conflates AIPAC name; fix spelling in book quotes.

jiang_claim: China is not a warrior culture; it would probably lose most wars; hard to get Chinese to fight.
mainstream_anchor: Chinese military history and modern PLA doctrine are extensive; contemporary strategic literature does not reduce China to non-warrior status.
mainstream_summary: Civilizational stereotype for classroom contrast; not acceptable as scholarly summary of Chinese strategic tradition.
evidence_notes: If book cites, balance with canonical Chinese military history and modern sources.

jiang_claim: Russia is the most corrupt society in all of Europe.
mainstream_anchor: Corruption indices (CPI, WGI) rank many countries; 'most corrupt in Europe' depends on definition of Europe and year — Russia scores very poorly but superlatives vary.
mainstream_summary: Directionally harsh; exact superlative should be sourced.
evidence_notes: Use Transparency International or World Bank series with date.

jiang_claim: NATO expanded five times after the Soviet Union fell; NATO was going to expand into Ukraine (as cause of war).
mainstream_anchor: NATO enlargement rounds and Ukraine's partnership/aspirations are documented; 'five times' and monocausal NATO→war claims are contested vs Russian decision-making and international law readings.
mainstream_summary: Geopolitical narrative compression; cite NATO fact sheets and serious IR scholarship for book.
evidence_notes: Pair with Ukrainian and Western legal positions if used.

jiang_claim: Operation Barbarossa and prewar posture are reframed as optimal for the USSR in game-theoretic terms — Stalin 'played' Hitler; standard school lesson ('Stalin trusted Hitler' / blunder) is wrong.
mainstream_anchor: Most Western and many Russian academic accounts emphasize German surprise attack, catastrophic early losses, intelligence failures, purges, and Stalin's misjudgments — not a deliberate optimal trap narrative.
mainstream_summary: Lecture offers a contrarian strategic reading; requires engagement with primary sources and specialist literature (Glantz, Gorodetsky, etc.) before book-level assertion.
evidence_notes: Separate pedagogical thought experiment from evidence claims; Lend-Lease magnitudes in lecture also need checking vs official statistics.

jiang_claim: The United States blew up the Nord Stream pipeline(s) (stated as fact in lecture).
mainstream_anchor: Attribution remains disputed in public investigations and reporting; no single consensus verdict in open sources as of typical coverage.
mainstream_summary: Treat as contested geopolitical claim, not settled fact, unless book cites specific investigations and their limits.
evidence_notes: Update if official findings become definitive; preserve transcript wording in archive regardless.

jiang_claim: About one quarter of young Americans now believe Osama bin Laden was a good guy (used to show myth collapse).
mainstream_anchor: Requires verification against named poll, question wording, and field dates; viral clips and misreads have circulated around similar themes.
mainstream_summary: Do not cite numerically in book without primary poll citation; label as lecture statistic pending check.
evidence_notes: Search Pew/YouGov/etc. for bin Laden favorability among youth if claim is retained.

jiang_claim: Israel right now is committing genocide in Gaza (and related youth-turn framing).
mainstream_anchor: Legal and scholarly usage of 'genocide' is contested and institution-specific (ICJ proceedings, UN bodies, historians); mainstream press wording varies.
mainstream_summary: Archive preserves lecture voice; book should distinguish legal claims, moral claims, and journalistic conventions.
evidence_notes: Pair with contemporaneous ICJ filings and humanitarian reporting if used seriously.

jiang_claim: Slavery abolition as the center explanation for the Civil War is false; main fight was federal power vs states / union preservation — Lincoln would end war without freeing slaves if he could.
mainstream_anchor: Academic consensus treats slavery as central to sectional conflict, war causation, and emancipation as evolving Union war aim; debates exist on emphasis but not as a simple either/or vs 'only union'.
mainstream_summary: Lecture uses Lincoln quotes and federal-power frame to minimize moral slavery narrative; book should cite Civil War historiography (Foner, McPherson, etc.).
evidence_notes: Not mutually exclusive with union-preservation and constitutional arguments; divergence is weighting.

jiang_claim: Four of the top five air forces in the world are U.S. military branches (Air Force, Navy, Army, Marines), with Russia third.
mainstream_anchor: Ranking depends on metric (total aircraft, combat-coded aircraft, tonnage, budget); meme-style lists circulate — serious defense accounting qualifies heavily.
mainstream_summary: Pedagogical punchline; verify against dated open-source military balance handbooks if cited.
evidence_notes: Often shared online; not a settled single ranking in specialist literature without definitions.

jiang_claim: No evidence COVID was actually dangerous; experimental vaccine pushed without trust justification (as part of institutional-trust collapse story).
mainstream_anchor: Public health consensus and vast literature on COVID morbidity/mortality and vaccine trials — strongly disputes lecture's framing.
mainstream_summary: Archive as lecture voice; do not treat as scientific summary.
evidence_notes: Highly polarized topic; if book addresses, cite WHO/CDC-era summaries with dates.

jiang_claim: November 2024 will see one of the lowest voter turnouts in American history because people give up on the system.
mainstream_anchor: Certified turnout in 2024 general election was historically high by US standards (common post-2020 pattern) — contradicts 'lowest' superlative unless narrowly defined.
mainstream_summary: Check FEC/state aggregates; lecture recorded before election — good candidate for resolved contradiction if using national turnout.
evidence_notes: Resolve with United States Election Project or similar series.

jiang_claim: Branch where Trump could blame 'the Jews' for tricking America into war (hypothetical inflammatory scenario).
mainstream_anchor: Classic antisemitic conspiracy pattern (Jewish collective responsibility / manipulation) — widely condemned; not a mainstream policy-analytic category.
mainstream_summary: Flag for editorial sensitivity; archive preserves lecture wording for research integrity.
evidence_notes: If excerpted for book, add context and ethical framing; ADL/IHRA-style definitions may apply.

jiang_claim: What people call AI does not really exist; it is supervised machine learning; 'AI is a scam' — no self-awareness, only iterative optimization of a human-given structure.
mainstream_anchor: Industry and academia use 'AI' broadly (including deep learning, generative models, agents); capabilities and hype are contested but the field is not reducible to one 2010s-era supervised-learning tutorial.
mainstream_summary: Pedagogical compression for high school class; update if book cites post-2023 LLM era.
evidence_notes: Core supervised-learning explanation can be accurate for many systems; 'scam' is rhetorical.

jiang_claim: Sociality / cohesion ranking: high in Japan and Germany; low in India, Brazil, and China (lecture examples).
mainstream_anchor: Cross-national trust and rule-following vary by indicator (WVS, World Bank governance, crime); national-character summaries are contested and can stereotype.
mainstream_summary: Illustrative classroom contrast; cite indices if used in scholarship.
evidence_notes: Directional claims may hold for some metrics in some years — verify with dated sources.

jiang_claim: AfD described as almost Nazi / near-Nazi in lecture's gloss of 2024 EP election.
mainstream_anchor: German domestic and EU institutions debate AfD classification, surveillance, and rhetoric; 'Nazi' analogies are politically loaded and institutionally sensitive.
mainstream_summary: Archive as lecture voice; book should use contemporary German/EU primary sources.
evidence_notes: Verbatim inflammatory label — handle with care in public copy.

jiang_claim: Easier explanation for Christianity's spread: co-opted by Roman Empire for control vs illiterate Jesus alone converting the world.
mainstream_anchor: Early Christian history is a large field (texts, archaeology, sociology of religion); 'Roman co-option' is one thread among many (Pauline mission, urban networks, imperial shifts).
mainstream_summary: Skeptical aside for class; not a substitute for patristic/NT scholarship.
evidence_notes: Pair with mainstream surveys of Christianization if retained.

jiang_claim: Putin (and great men) framed via telepathy metaphor — 'almost telepath' as explanatory device for political skill.
mainstream_anchor: Political science emphasizes institutions, resources, coercion, and contingency; telepathy is not a literal explanatory variable.
mainstream_summary: Literary bridge from Foundation's Second Foundation; label as metaphor in any serious text.
evidence_notes: Connects to great-man theory edge-case discussion in lecture.

jiang_claim: The lecture treats the standard classroom story—farming as an obvious improvement that straightforwardly yields surplus, cities, literacy, and modernity—as poorly supported by accumulating evidence.
mainstream_anchor: Archaeology and synthesis (regional Neolithic studies, health and workload evidence) complicate a simple progressivist arc, but many scholars still trace major scalar change through sedentism, domestication, and food production without dismissing material and demographic shifts.
mainstream_summary: Divergence is weighting and rhetorical sharpness; book layer should name regional variation and peer-reviewed nuance alongside the lecture's corrective.
evidence_notes: Pair with surveys of Neolithic health indicators and sedentism-first sites if retained in print.

jiang_claim: The lecture frames the history taught in school and commonly believed as largely false—implanted in citizens' minds by powerful actors—so that discovering 'secret history' is a liberation move.
mainstream_anchor: History education and historiography discuss nationalism, archives, curriculum politics, and contested narratives without reducing standard history to uniform falsity or a single implantation mechanism; claims vary strongly by jurisdiction and period.
mainstream_summary: Treat as rhetorical thesis for class; book copy should separate pedagogical provocation from accredited historical method and cite curriculum studies where used.
evidence_notes: Pair with named work on public history and textbook politics if retained beyond lecture voice.

jiang_claim: The opening survey includes blunt biological and racial caricatures as examples of 'theories people believe' about human motivation.
mainstream_anchor: Contemporary social science and ethics reject race essentialism and treat gender/sexuality pedagogy with care; evolutionary accounts of behavior are contested, plural, and rarely taught as classroom stereotypes.
mainstream_summary: Archive as provocation-with-disclaimer; book layer needs explicit framing, content notes, and citations if any caricature is retained beyond lecture voice.
evidence_notes: High sensitivity topic — align with humane-purpose and minor-audience guardrails for any public copy.
